parent.name: Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton

children.name: Bertram de Verdun of Great Moulton

zh.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton是Ives de Verdun of Moulton和Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton的兒子，於大約1115年出生在艾歐洛。他於1145年之前與Mrs de Verdun of Moulton在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton是Ives de Verdun of Moulton和Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton的儿子，于大约1115年出生在艾歐洛。他于1145年之前与Mrs de Verdun of Moulton在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Moulton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Molden
meanings: English:
metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Mold, a form of Maud, with the characteristically West Midland genitival suffix -en.
variant of   Moulton  .

variants.name: Moulden
meanings: English: variant of   Moulton  .

variants.name: Molton
meanings: English: variant of   Moulton  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a Norman baronial name from any of various places so called from Gaulish elements meaning ‘place of alders’ (  compare  Vere  ).

variants.name: Varden
meanings: Anglo-Norman: Wardeyn, a guard, a keeper of a gate

en.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton was born about 1115, in Europe as the son of Ives de Verdun of Moulton and Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. He married Mrs de Verdun of Moulton before 1145, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton est né environ 1115, à Europe, fils de Ives de Verdun of Moulton et Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. Il a épousé Mrs de Verdun of Moulton avant 1145, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton wurde um 1115 in Europa, als Sohn von Ives de Verdun of Moulton und Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton geboren. Er hat vor 1145 in Europa Mrs de Verdun of Moulton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton è nato il circa 1115 nel Europa come figlio di Ives de Verdun of Moulton e Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. Lui sposato Mrs de Verdun of Moulton prima del 1145, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ja.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moultonは約1115年にヨーロッパでIves de Verdun of MoultonとMrs Ives de Verdun of Moultonの息子として生まれた。1145年以前にヨーロッパでMrs de Verdun of Moultonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton(은)는 대략 1115년 우럽에서 Ives de Verdun of Moulton(와)과 Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1145년 이전 우럽에서 Mrs de Verdun of Moulton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton nasceu aproximadamente 1115, em Europa filho de Ives de Verdun of Moulton e Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. Ele casou-se com Mrs de Verdun of Moulton antes 1145, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton родился около 1115 г. в местности Европа. Его родителей звали Ives de Verdun of Moulton и Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs de Verdun of Moulton перед 1145 г. в местности Европа. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: de Verdun of Moulton nació aproximadamente 1115, en Europa como hijo de Ives de Verdun of Moulton y Mrs Ives de Verdun of Moulton. Se casó con Mrs de Verdun of Moulton antes de 1145, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
